Figure 3. CCR7 modulates the response to hypoosmotic stimulation via PIEZO1 1001 (A) Knockdown of IP receptor 3 (ITPR3) does not affect the calcium response to 1002 hypotonic stimulation. GCaMP8f fluorescence traces of HEK293T cells expressing 1003 sgControl or sgITPR3 show similar calcium transients upon hypotonic treatment, 1004 measured by microplate reader. Traces represent mean F/F ± SEM (n = 8 biological 1005 replicates). ns, not significant. Unpaired two tailed Student's t-test. 1006

Figure 4. CCR7 modulates PIEZO1-mediated calcium influx through the Gαs 1036 -cAMP-PKA pathway 1037

Figure 2. CaMPARI2-based CRISPRi screen reveals known and novel regulators 966 of osmomechanical signaling 967 (A) Schematic of the CaMPARI2-based pooled CRISPRi screening workflow. 968 HEK293T cells stably expressing CRISPRi machinery (dCas9–KRAB) and 969 CaMPARI2 were transduced with a lentiviral sgRNA library targeting 2,418 970 membrane-associated genes (H6 library). Following selection and expansion, cells 971 were subjected to hypotonic stimulation with 3 min violet light (405 nm) illumination 972 to induce CaMPARI2 photoconversion. Cells with low CaMPARI2 R/G ratios 973 (bottom 15%) were isolated by FACS, and sgRNA representation in input and sorted 974 populations was quantified by next-generation sequencing (NGS) to identify genes 975 regulating osmomechanical calcium responses. 976
